evidence_citation: GWAS Catalog mapped_gene (nearest gene by position); dbSNP rs12913832
notes: Intron variant within HERC2; nearest-gene label, NOT the functional target.

evidence_citation: PMID:22234890 (Visser, Kayser & Palstra 2012, Genome Res 22:446-455, doi:10.1101/gr.128652.111)
notes: rs12913832 sits in a HERC2 intron ~21 kb from OCA2; allele attenuates a long-range enhancer / chromatin loop regulating OCA2 transcription. Established in forensic/European pigmentation literature; in the Ang2023 Kalinago cohort this SNP was NOT genome-wide significant (P_adj=0.075) - the regulatory mechanism is the cited external finding, not a Kalinago result.

OCA2 R305W missense; positional label == functional gene here (coding variant in OCA2). Scores as pathogenic by SIFT/PolyPhen2/PANTHER (CADD 22.7) yet shows no detectable pigmentation effect once NW273KV is controlled (P_adj=0.981). This is the D1 anchor (genotype-present, phenotype-absent): predicted-pathogenic variant, phenotype absent.

evidence_citation: Ang2023 eLife 10.7554/eLife.77514 (Table 2, exon 8 MNP); dbSNP rs797044784
notes: OCA2 NW273KV novel multi-nucleotide albinism allele; causal in Kalinago (per-allele -7.7 melanin-index units, p=2.2e-5). This is the D2 counterpart (phenotype-present, genotype-absent for the CATALOGUED variants): albinism traces to a population-specific novel variant a standard OCA2 panel would miss.
